stories.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
stories.author: Cyrus Farivar
bullet_points: A survivor of a school shooting is suing an AI gun detection firm.
The lawsuit alleges the AI system failed to detect a weapon during the incident.
The story raises critical questions about the required accuracy and reliability of AI in high-stakes public safety applications.

stories.title: How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work: Autonomy, Efficiency, and Scope
stories.author: Jerry Ma
bullet_points: AI systems are evolving from conversational assistants to autonomous agents capable of end-to-end task execution.
The research empirically examines how these AI agents are accelerating and reshaping knowledge work.
The study utilizes production data from Perplexity's Search and Computer products to draw three key findings.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing performance for Apple users.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or via the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A small modular nuclear reactor (SMR) has achieved criticality in its initial test.
The reactor technology originates from a startup named Antares.
The unit is currently not operational for power generation, despite reaching criticality.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking or restricting passive investment access for SpaceX.
The exclusion or restriction also extends to major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or controversial tech companies are integrated into traditional investment indices.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the token explosion and attention dilution issues in long-video understanding for Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
The framework decouples perception and reasoning by transforming long-video analysis into an agentic exploration process.
It utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction, allowing for incremental video processing.

stories.title: Act As a Real Researcher: A Suite of Benchmarks Evaluating Frontier LLMs and Agentic Harnesses in Research Lifecycle
stories.author: Xiangyong Cao
bullet_points: New benchmarks evaluate frontier LLMs and agentic harnesses across the entire research lifecycle.
Agents show proficiency in complex tasks like coding and autonomous experiment execution.
Current limitations in field sensitivity, ethics, and scientific judgment prevent agents from fully replacing human researchers.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the token explosion and attention dilution issues in long video understanding for Vision-Language Models.
It decouples perception and reasoning by framing long-video understanding as an agentic exploration process.
The framework utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for improved functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: NASA is collaborating with Prada on advanced astronaut gear for the Artemis IV mission.
The focus is on the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG), the base layer of the spacesuit.
This technology is crucial for maintaining astronaut comfort and life support during lunar missions in 2028.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical user interaction.
The security concern relates to the functionality of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The product seller does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs on discrete probability problems to assess probabilistic reasoning.
Models perform highly on standard probability exercises (0.96 accuracy) but struggle with counterintuitive problems (0.59 accuracy).
The research evaluates the impact of Chain-of-Thought prompting across eight state-of-the-art models.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, where new learning causes catastrophic forgetting.
Introduces SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to resolve this conflict.
The framework uses a Mixture of Sparse Experts to differentiate between task-specific knowledge and shared model capabilities.

stories.title: Whisper Hallucination Detection and Mitigation via Hidden Representation Steering and Sparse AutoEncoders
stories.author: Assel Yermekova
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucinations' in the widely used Whisper ASR model.
Proposes using internal representations (raw activations and Sparse AutoEncoders) for detection and mitigation.
Demonstrates that these representation spaces successfully encode linearly separable information related to hallucinations.

stories.title: Planning-aligned Token Compression for Long-Context Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of monolithic vision-action models in autonomous driving when handling long temporal contexts.
Proposes 'Planning-aligned Token Compression' as a method to reduce token sequence size without modifying the core model architecture.
Identifies the need for more advanced compression techniques beyond existing rule-based methods for real-time performance.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, where new learning causes catastrophic forgetting.
Proposes SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to resolve this conflict.
The framework uses a Mixture of Sparse Experts to differentiate between task-specific knowledge and shared model capabilities.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: Traditional HPC workflows are deterministic and linear, contrasting with modern AI-driven paradigms.
The integration of AI and foundation models is shifting scientific computation towards iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic workflows.
The paper addresses the unique computational challenges introduced by moving from deterministic to probabilistic AI-driven HPC workflows.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs' probabilistic reasoning by testing them on discrete probability problems.
Models show high accuracy (0.96) on standard probability exercises but significantly drop to 0.59 on counterintuitive problems.
The research evaluates 8 state-of-the-art models, comparing performance with and without Chain-of-Thought prompting.

stories.title: Agentopia: Long-Term Life Simulation and Learning in Agent Societies
stories.author: Yunzhe Tao
bullet_points: The research explores using LLM-powered agents to simulate long-term social life and learning, mirroring human social development.
A key limitation addressed is the short time scale of previous agent society simulations, which restricted deep social interaction.
The study investigates whether LLMs can gain a better understanding of human behavior through extended simulated social experience.

stories.title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
stories.author: Rui Yan
bullet_points: LLMs show strong zero-shot capabilities but perform poorly as standalone embedding models.
The research suggests text embeddings align too closely with frequent but uninformative tokens in the vocabulary space.
The paper proposes that the 'UnEmbedding Matrix' acts as a feature lens, potentially explaining the embedding deficiency.

stories.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
stories.author: Bernt Schiele
bullet_points: Introduces TEVI, a new framework designed to improve vision-language alignment in models like CLIP.
Addresses the issue of information imbalance where image embeddings contain more detail than their corresponding text captions.
Uses captions as a targeted signal to guide and refine (edit) the visual representations within the model.

stories.title: Sycophantic Praise: Evaluating Excessive Praise in Language Models
stories.author: Tianyu Jiang
bullet_points: Addresses the under-researched area of 'sycophantic praise' in Language Models (LLMs), distinguishing it from simple agreement.
Proposes a novel, parameterized framework to quantify whether praise is excessive relative to the actual contribution quality.
Demonstrates that the new framework significantly outperforms existing generic LLM judgment methods for this specific alignment problem.

stories.title: The Lipreading Gap: Do VSR Models Perceive Visual Speech Like Human Lipreaders?
stories.author: Naomi Harte
bullet_points: Visual Speech Recognition (VSR) models are outperforming human lipreaders on current benchmarks.
The study investigates whether this performance translates to human-like visual speech perception by comparing VSR systems to human baselines.
Analysis shows that while models have higher overall accuracy, their successes and failures differ from human performance across word, character, and phoneme levels.

stories.title: The Masked Advantage: Uncovering Local-Language Access to Cultural Knowledge in LLMs
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Investigates whether local cultural knowledge is better accessed by LLMs in English versus the local language.
Identifies limitations in current evaluations, such as reliance on rigid, template-based questions.
Proposes a methodology to decouple general language proficiency from language-conditioned cultural knowledge access.

stories.title: M$^3$Exam: Benchmarking Multimodal Memory for Realistic User-Agent Interactions
stories.author: Xiaofang Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces M$^3$Exam, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal conversational memory in language agents.
Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks which assume simplistic, human-human interactions.
Focuses on realistic user-agent interactions, including reasoning over authentic multimodal file content and interpreting concealed user information.

stories.title: I Built an Adversarial Eval Framework and Attacked 5 LLMs — Every Single One Failed
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: An adversarial evaluation framework was built to test the robustness of several Large Language Models (LLMs).
The framework tested 10 adversarial scenarios against models including Llama, Qwen, and GPT-OSS.
All tested LLMs performed poorly, with none scoring above 63% in the evaluation.
